Title: Neofunctionalization of an OMT cluster dominates polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis associated with the domestication of citrus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Clementine; Mangshan mandarin and comparative citrus accessions
Common_Genes.Promoters: native promoter; comparative promoter sequence analysis only
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Correlated with total PMF content during fruit development in C. reticulata; negligible in Group I and II species lacking OMT cluster; lower than OMT cluster relevance for PMF variation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Fruit flavedo/developing fruit associated expression in mandarins
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, UV/pathogen barrier
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flavedo; also leaves and flowers in citrus
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OMT6 homologs contribute limited or weak hydroxyflavone O-methylation in most citrus lineages; ancestral/related reference for emergence of PMF biosynthesis rather than main determinant of natural PMF variation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning from multiple citrus accessions; recombinant protein assays with hydroxyflavone substrates; expression–metabolite correlation; comparative genomics/synteny; site-directed mutagenesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as evolutionary reference and template for engineering neofunctionalized OMT activity, but not primary breeding target compared with OMT cluster
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly homologous to CreOMT3/4/5 (91.2–95.5% identity); likely ancestral source of tandem OMT cluster via duplication; D17N substitution confers new multisite activity including C3/C5/C6/C3' methylation; AbOMT6 has strong catalytic capacity but is not expressed in primitive citrus
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT6 is closely related to the OMT cluster and likely ancestral to CreOMT3/4/5. 2. Most OMT6 homologs lack the full multisite activity needed for efficient PMF biosynthesis, explaining weak PMF accumulation outside mandarins. 3. Amino acid variation, especially residue 17, helps explain functional divergence between OMT6 and CreOMT4.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: 35S/constitutive overexpression construct in callus and transient fruit assays (vector-based, exact promoter not explicitly stated for all assays)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Strongly up-regulated in PMF-rich fruit flavedo; highly correlated with total PMFs and six PMF monomers; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: High in flavedo during fruit development in PMF-rich mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Polymethoxyflavone biosynthesis / flavone O-methylation branch of flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal hydroxyflavone O-methylation steps leading to citrus PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to specific hydroxyl groups on hydroxyflavones, especially C6 and C4' and also C3' and C7 on some substrates, contributing to sequential production of PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including DMF1, 3'-DNOB, GEN, CIR, LYS and other tested hydroxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Various O-methylated flavones and PMF intermediates/products including NOB, SIN, TAN, TMF2, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms PMF products and converts PMF intermediates toward fully O-methylated flavones
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant for PMF diversification and accumulation; not explicitly established as single rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, UV/pathogen defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PMF accumulation via multisite O-methylation of hydroxyflavones; overexpression increases total and specific PMFs, while RNAi lowers them
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcriptome–metabolome correlation; yeast feeding assays; recombinant enzyme kinetics; citrus callus overexpression; transient fruit flavedo overexpression; RNAi suppression; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CreOMT4; CreOMT5 (functional complementation in combined reactions)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue specificity (flavedo vs juice sac); developmental stage; domestication background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for PMF biofortification and pathway reconstruction; useful in gene stacking with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5 for enhanced PMF production in citrus or microbes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Efficiently methylates C6 and C4' sites; acts complementarily with CreOMT4 and CreOMT5 to fully O-methylate flavones; under purifying selection compared with OMT4/5
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT3 maps to the major PMF locus and its expression tightly correlates with PMF accumulation. 2. The enzyme catalyzes regioselective multisite O-methylation, especially at C6 and C4'. 3. Overexpression increases PMFs and RNAi decreases PMFs, proving causal contribution to citrus PMF biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Clementine; Mangshan mandarin; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native OMT4 promoters compared: CreOMT4-Pro (738 bp, short promoter) and CrmOMT4-P1 (1,779 bp, long promoter with 1,041-bp MITE-containing fragment); transient overexpression constructs also used
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in PMF-rich flavedo and strongly correlated with total PMFs; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins; promoter deletion reduces expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: High in flavedo and during developmental stages associated with PMF accumulation in mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal hydroxyflavone O-methylation steps leading to major citrus PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to hydroxyflavones at multiple positions including C3, C5, C6, C7 and C3', enabling systematic O-methylation toward PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3HF, GAD, DMF1, LYS, 3'-DNOB, GEN, CIR and others
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple methylated flavones and PMFs including NOB, SIN, TAN, SAL, TMF2, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms PMF products and converts intermediate PMFs such as 5DNOB toward NOB
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control enzyme for natural PMF variation; major terminal determinant supported by genetics and promoter polymorphism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, UV/pathogen defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes PMF biosynthesis; promoter variation and catalytic neofunctionalization make it a decisive contributor to high PMF accumulation in wild mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcriptome–metabolome correlation; recombinant enzyme kinetics; yeast assays; site-directed mutagenesis; transient overexpression; RNAi suppression; dual-luciferase promoter assay; genotyping of promoter deletion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; domestication status; promoter structural variation with MITE-containing fragment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Modern domestication selected lower-expression allele associated with reduced PMF content; no agronomic penalty directly reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top breeding target for marker-assisted selection using 1,041-bp promoter fragment; prime enzyme for PMF biofortification and metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: N17 is essential for C3/C5/C6/C3' methylation; N17D abolishes most activity, while reciprocal D17N in OMT6 confers broader activity; long promoter with 1,041-bp fragment causes ~3-fold higher LUC activity than short cultivated promoter
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT4 is a multifunctional OMT central to PMF biosynthesis in mandarin. 2. Its N17 residue is essential for broad multisite methylation and explains neofunctionalization relative to OMT6. 3. A 1,041-bp MITE-containing promoter fragment elevates expression and underlies higher PMF accumulation in wild and early-admixture mandarins.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: Red tangerine; Guoqing No.1 satsuma mandarin; Ota Ponkan mandarin
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Constitutive/transient overexpression constructs used; native promoter context analyzed comparatively
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Strongly up-regulated in PMF-rich flavedo; highly correlated with total PMFs and multiple PMF monomers; higher in wild and early-admixture mandarins than in modern cultivated mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: High in flavedo during PMF accumulation in mandarins
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal hydroxyflavone O-methylation steps leading to major citrus PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to hydroxyflavones at C3, C5, C6, C7 and especially C3' on certain substrates, converting hydroxyflavone intermediates into PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxyflavones including 3HF, GAD, DMF1, LYS, 3'-DNOB, CIR and other hydroxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Multiple methylated flavones and PMFs including NOB, SIN, TAN, SAL, TMF2, API-TMF, 8-OH-HXMF
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms PMF products and converts intermediate PMFs such as 5DNOB/3'-DNOB toward NOB and related PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant for PMF formation; not explicitly isolated as sole bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular protection, prevention of metabolic disorders, UV/pathogen defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of multiple PMFs through complementary multisite O-methylation; OE elevates PMFs and RNAi reduces PMFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; transcriptome–metabolome correlation; yeast feeding assays; recombinant enzyme kinetics; citrus callus overexpression; transient fruit overexpression; RNAi suppression; HPLC profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CreOMT3; CreOMT4 (functional complementation in combined reactions)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit tissue specificity; developmental stage; domestication background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking target with CreOMT3 and CreOMT4 for high-PMF citrus breeding and microbial PMF production systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows strong C3' activity toward 3'-DNOB and broad C5/C7 activity; neofunctionalized relative to OMT6 by possessing C3-O-methylation activity; under positive selection
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSA pool sequencing: PRJNA938971; RNA-seq: PRJNA341533; PRJNA579049; PRJNA776912
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CreOMT5 is a multifunctional terminal OMT whose expression tightly tracks PMF accumulation. 2. The enzyme efficiently methylates several hydroxyflavones, notably catalyzing strong C3' methylation of 3'-DNOB. 3. Overexpression and RNAi demonstrate its causal role in raising or lowering citrus PMF levels.
